papers.title_en: A Theoretical Analysis of Memory and Overfitting Phenomena in Stochastic Interpolation Models
papers.authors: Yunchen Li, Shaohui Lin, Zhou Yu
papers.evidence: 理论分析了生成建模中随机插值模型的记忆现象
papers.tldr: 随机插值模型中的记忆与过拟合现象缺乏理论分析。本文通过推导最优速度场和得分函数的闭式解，揭示了连续时间下生成过程能完美恢复训练样本；在欧拉离散化下，生成样本围绕训练样本波动且偏差受步长控制。进一步分析发现，估计误差累积会导致生成样本偏离训练集。最终将生成样本分解为训练样本加上离散化误差、估计误差和高斯噪声三项扰动，并据此给出过拟合与欠拟合的理论定义。该工作为理解生成模型的记忆行为提供了理论框架。
papers.abstract_en: This paper provides a theoretical account of memorization in stochastic interpolation models. By leveraging closed-form expressions for the optimal velocity field and the associated score function, we show that, in the continuous-time oracle setting, both deterministic and stochastic generation processes recover training samples. Under Euler discretization, generated samples remain centered around training samples, with deviations controlled by the step size. We further analyze generation in the presence of estimation errors and show that accumulated estimation errors control the endpoint deviation from the training set. These results imply that the generated sample admits a representation as a training sample perturbed by three controlled terms: a discretization-induced bound, an estimation-error-induced bound, and stochastic Gaussian noise. Based on this characterization, we provide theoretical definitions of overfitting and underfitting in generative models. Synthetic simulations support our theoretical findings.
